_description: All VALENCE model weights. Every parameter has a type (literature/expert/prior) and justification. Change this file to recalibrate the model; pipeline.py reads it on every run.

trs._description: Task Replaceability Score = probability of significant structural transformation within 10 years (>30% task-hours redistributed)

routine_cog.source: Frey & Osborne 2013; Acemoglu & Restrepo 2018 — routine cognitive task share is the primary predictor of AI substitutability
routine_cog.justification: Highest prior weight: routine cognitive work is directly and most reliably measured by O*NET and is the clearest substitution signal

digitizability.justification: Digital output is prerequisite for AI substitution; physical presence reduces but does not eliminate digitizability

social_perceptiveness_inverse.source: Frey & Osborne 2013 — social intelligence as substitution barrier
social_perceptiveness_inverse.justification: Inverse of social perceptiveness: high social skill = harder to automate

prior.justification: Majority weight to structural prior: AEI measures Claude usage patterns, not economy-wide AI deployment. Prior is more generalisable.

aei_observed.justification: Significant but minority weight: AEI provides direct observed signal but is biased toward Claude-using organisations

uncertainty_sigma.justification: Approximate 1σ from weight uncertainty; not a formal confidence interval. Treat as order-of-magnitude uncertainty bound.

dr._description: Displacement Resistance = institutional, physical, and legal friction preventing human removal after capability threshold is met

licensing.justification: Highest shell weight: licensing is the most direct and legally enforceable barrier to AI substitution

physical.source: Frey & Osborne 2013 — manual dexterity and physical proximity as substitution barriers; O*NET scale CX
physical.justification: Physical presence requirement directly blocks digital-first AI substitution

union.justification: Union density proxies collective resistance to displacement and bargaining power over AI adoption

sovereign.source: Expert judgment — statutory authority and named liability as institutional barriers
sovereign.justification: Sovereign authority (judicial, public safety, constitutional function) creates displacement barriers beyond market forces

credential.justification: Depth of specialised expertise proxy; lower weight because it partially overlaps with licensing

pvci._description: Platform Value Capture Index = share of productivity gains flowing to platform/infrastructure owners rather than workers

digitizability.justification: Digital output is prerequisite for platform mediation; highest PVCI weight

platform_sw_ratio.source: O*NET hot technology share of total software skills
platform_sw_ratio.justification: Hot technology dependency proxies platform lock-in

comp_mediation.source: O*NET Work Context: Interacting with Computers
comp_mediation.justification: Computer mediation degree indicates output routability through platform

switching_cost.justification: Proprietary software dependency proxy. Future: estimate from firm-level spend concentration.

output_portability.justification: Inverse of physical presence: digital output is portable and platform-capturable

market_concentration.justification: Assumed 0.60 (moderate concentration) for all occupations. Future: BLS Economic Census HHI.

market_concentration_prior.justification: No firm-level HHI data available. Moderate concentration assumed as conservative estimate.

family_gates._description: Column (family) assignment: 8-gate decision tree applied in order. First gate that fires wins.

noble_gas.justification: Both licensing AND sovereign authority must be near-maximum for noble gas classification. Strong binary institutional facts.

care_trust.justification: Licensed + high physical + low sovereign = relational protection not institutional authority

bottleneck.justification: Licensed + moderate sovereign = accountability bottleneck without full noble gas protection

halogen.justification: High capture pull + no institutional protection = platform capture magnet
halogen._note: Raised from 0.35 to 0.40 to exclude physical occupations (Machinist, Warehouse, Stock Clerk, Sales Agent) whose O*NET software ratios are high due to tool use, not digitizable output. Ca and Sd are Halogens via domain_overrides.

network_former.justification: High computer mediation + low routine + low protection = organisational hub

rapid_substituter.justification: reactivity = routine_cog × (1 - shell_score). High reactivity + low shells = fast substitution.

elasticity_no_intervention.source: Acemoglu & Restrepo 2022 — displacement elasticity upper bound

elasticity_proactive_policy.source: Acemoglu & Restrepo 2022 — lower bound with worker-centred institutions

org_transition_threshold.justification: Expert judgment: below 30% enterprise adoption = early adopter phase; above = mainstream deployment

lir._description: Lock-in Risk = composite risk that value concentration becomes self-reinforcing
lir.justification: Equal weight to capability and capture risk (TRS, PVCI); displacement resistance dampens; employment scale amplifies systemic risk

confidence._description: Confidence score based on evidence quality, recency, coverage, and source diversity

iceberg.note: Occupation-level iceberg scores are VALENCE estimates using Iceberg methodology (Chopra et al., 2025). Not official Project Iceberg outputs.
